page: Microsoft Azure E20d_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the EDv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 10 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 160.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Additionally, it includes 768 GB of local SSD storage, while containing no dedicated GPUs.

Benchmark evaluations place the E20d_v4 in the average performance tier across most compute and memory operations, with a notable exception in memory performance. The PassMark memory score is classified as strong, placing in the top 25% with a score of 2791.74. This is supported by a read-only small block memory bandwidth of 1037.19 GB/sec. Large block uncached memory bandwidth reaches 90.14 GB/sec for reads and 45.31 GB/sec for writes. In compute-heavy tasks, the server achieves a PassMark CPU score of 19245.76 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 18627.31. Large language model inference using a medium 7B model shows a weak prompt processing speed of 29.55 tokens/sec, though text generation remains average at 10.2 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E20d_v4 offers a high-density memory configuration paired with local storage, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require specialized GPU acceleration. The primary tradeoff is its average single-core and multi-core CPU compute capabilities, which may limit its effectiveness for highly parallelized, compute-bound tasks. However, the combination of 160.0 GB of RAM, a strong memory subsystem, and 768 GB of local storage makes this server suitable for database operations, memory-cached web serving, and enterprise applications requiring large memory footprints.
description: Microsoft Azure E20d_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the EDv4 family, powered by a 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs (10 physical cores with hyper-threading), 160.0 GB of RAM, and 768 GB of local storage, with no integrated GPUs. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the server exhibits strong memory performance, scoring in the top 25% on PassMark memory benchmarks with a read-only small block bandwidth of 1037.19 GB/sec. Other benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core (18627.31) and static web serving (14.26 GB/sec), fall within the average tier. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core makes this server qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for database operations, caching layers, and enterprise applications that require substantial memory capacity and local storage without demanding elite compute performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20d_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 20 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 160.0 GB of RAM, and 768 GB of local storage, delivering strong memory performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20d_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 20 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 160.0 GB of RAM, and 768 GB of local SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local SSD storage for memory-intensive database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 20 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8272CL processors
160.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
768 GB of local SSD storage for fast data access
Strong memory performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Suited for database operations, caching, and memory-heavy enterprise applications
